title: Stimulation of MHC class I binding receptors on decidual NK clones.
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"n86w7","text":"We used anti-KIR2DS4 mAb (RnD systems), anti-LIR1 mab (HPF1 clone, kindly provided by Dr. Miguel Lopez-Botet) and EB6 mAb directed against KIR2DL1 and KIR2DS1 (Immunotech, Westbrook, ME).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Perform in vitro culture and generation of IL-2 dNK clones as previously described [1,2].
Characterize NK clones for their expression of KIR2DS4, LIR-1 and KIR2DL1 receptors.

Select clones that were positive for only one of these receptors for co-incubation experiments. Among EB6 positive clones, select only the clones on which functional expression of KIR2DL1 was verified by inhibition of killing Cw4 and Cw6 transfected 721.221 target cells 28.

The MHC class I negative cell line 721.221 has been previously used to generate transfectants with ligands for a number of activating and inhibitory KIR receptors (721.221-Cw4, 721.221-Cw6, 721.221-HLA-G) 2,3. We performed co-culturing experiments of 30 X 10 3 dNK cells per well from each clone expressing a certain characterized receptor profile with an equal amount of different irradiated 721.221 transfectant cells for 72 hours at 37 o C 5% CO 2 in Bio-MPM-1 medium with 5% pen-strep and non-essential amino acids, followed by ELISA measurement of produced cytokines.
Decidual NK cells regulate key developmental processes at the human fetal-maternal interface by Hanna, J. et al. published in Nature Medicine on 09 Jun, 2006 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nm1452
creator: Protocols Importer
